Name: Necklaced Woodpecker
Habitat: Subtropical or tropical moist montane forests
Best_seen_at: Mishmi Hills, Namdapha National Park (Arunachal Pradesh)
How_to_identify: Small woodpecker with black upperparts and white spotting. Distinctive crimson patch on the breast and a black 'necklace' or band across the upper breast. Male has a red crown; female has a black crown.
Diet: Insects and larvae found in mossy trees

Name: Rufous-bellied Woodpecker
Habitat: Broadleaf and coniferous forests, especially oak and rhododendron
Best_seen_at: Sattal (Uttarakhand), Great Himalayan National Park (Himachal Pradesh)
How_to_identify: Striking rufous-brown underparts and face. Upperparts are black with heavy white barring. Male has a full red crown; female has a black crown with white spots.
Diet: Tree sap, insects, and occasionally fruit

Name: Fulvous-breasted Woodpecker
Habitat: Open forests, orchards, and urban gardens
Best_seen_at: Jim Corbett National Park (Uttarakhand), Kaziranga National Park (Assam)
How_to_identify: Buff/fulvous underparts with light streaking and a red vent. Upperparts are black with white barring. Male has a red crown; female has a black crown. Lacks the heavy breast spotting of the Freckle-breasted.

Name: Freckle-breasted Woodpecker
How_to_identify: Very similar to Fulvous-breasted but has distinct dark spots (freckles) on the breast and a paler face. In India, it is primarily found in the Andaman islands.

Name: Stripe-breasted Woodpecker
How_to_identify: Heavily black-striped underparts and a deep red vent. Upperparts are black with white bars. Male has a red crown; female has a black crown. More heavily marked than the Fulvous-breasted.

Name: Darjeeling Woodpecker
How_to_identify: Large woodpecker with a yellowish wash on the neck sides and breast. Underparts are heavily streaked. Large white wing patches. Male has a red nape patch; female lacks it.
Diet: Insects and larvae extracted from decaying wood

Name: Himalayan Woodpecker
How_to_identify: Black upperparts with large white scapular patches. Underparts are plain buffy-white with a red vent. Male has a crimson crown; female has a black crown.
Diet: Insects, seeds (especially conifer cones), and nuts

Name: Sind Woodpecker
Habitat: Arid riverine scrub, thorn forests, and desert oases
How_to_identify: Pale-looking woodpecker. Similar to Himalayan Woodpecker but smaller and occurs in arid habitats. Has more extensive white on the wings and tail. Male has a red crown; female has a black crown.
Diet: Insects and larvae found in desert scrub

Name: Great Spotted Woodpecker
How_to_identify: Rare vagrant or restricted resident in the extreme north. Black upperparts with large white wing patches. Bright red vent. Male has a red spot on the nape; female has a black nape; juveniles have a red crown.
Diet: Insects, seeds, and occasionally bird eggs

Name: White-bellied Woodpecker
Habitat: Primary evergreen and moist deciduous forests
How_to_identify: One of India's largest woodpeckers. Predominantly black with a white belly and white rump. Male has a full red crest and red moustachial stripe; female has red only on the hindcrest.
Diet: Large grubs, termites, and ants found in old-growth trees
